annotations.label: Polynomial interpolation

anchor.snippet: polynomial interpolation is the interpolation of a given data set by the polynomial of lowest possible degree

annotations.note: `Lagrange.interpolate s v r` produces the unique polynomial of degree `< #s` interpolating the given data.

annotations.label: Interpolating polynomial

anchor.snippet: is said to interpolate the data if

annotations.note: `eval_interpolate_at_node` states the interpolation property `eval (v i) (interpolate s v r) = r i`.

annotations.label: Existence and uniqueness (lead)

anchor.snippet: There is always a unique such polynomial, commonly given by two explicit formulas

annotations.note: `eq_interpolate_iff` characterises the interpolant as the unique polynomial of degree `< #s` taking the prescribed values.

annotations.label: Interpolation theorem

anchor.snippet: there exists a unique polynomial

annotations.note: Existence is given by `Lagrange.interpolate` and uniqueness by `eq_interpolate_iff`.

annotations.label: Interpolation as linear bijection

anchor.snippet: polynomial interpolation defines a linear bijection

annotations.note: `funEquivDegreeLT` is the linear equivalence between `degreeLT F #s` and `s → F` via evaluation/interpolation.

annotations.label: Validity over infinite fields

anchor.snippet: The theorem is also valid over any infinite field in place of the real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Lagrange.interpolate` is stated over an arbitrary `Field F`, subsuming the infinite-field case.

annotations.label: Lagrange basis functions

anchor.snippet: Consider the Lagrange basis functions

annotations.note: `Lagrange.basis s v i` is the polynomial that evaluates to 1 at `v i` and 0 at the other nodes.

annotations.label: Lagrange combination interpolates

anchor.snippet: is an interpolating polynomial of degree

annotations.note: Combined with `Lagrange.degree_interpolate_lt`, this proves the Lagrange combination interpolates with degree `< #s`.

annotations.label: Uniqueness via zeros

anchor.snippet: a non-zero polynomial of degree at most

annotations.note: A polynomial of degree `< #s` vanishing on `s` distinct points must be zero.

annotations.label: Vandermonde invertibility

anchor.snippet: The matrix X on the left is a Vandermonde matrix

annotations.note: `det_vandermonde` gives the product formula for the Vandermonde determinant, implying invertibility at distinct nodes.

annotations.label: Corollary: interpolation of low-degree polynomial

anchor.snippet: the interpolating polynomial of

annotations.note: `eq_interpolate` shows that any polynomial of degree `< #s` equals its own Lagrange interpolant.

annotations.label: Lagrange interpolation formula

anchor.snippet: We may write down the polynomial immediately in terms of Lagrange polynomials

annotations.note: `interpolate_eq_sum` gives the explicit Lagrange formula as the sum of `C (r i / ∏(v i - v j)) * ∏(X - C (v j))`.

annotations.label: Newton interpolation theorem

anchor.snippet: that interpolates

annotations.note: No Newton form of the interpolating polynomial appears in Mathlib (searches for Newton interpolation and divided differences return nothing relevant).

annotations.label: Divided differences / Newton coefficients

anchor.snippet: is the notation for divided differences

annotations.note: No divided-difference notion appears under that name or related search in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Newton forward interpolation formula

anchor.snippet: the Newton forward interpolation formula is expressed as

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Newton forward interpolation formula.

annotations.label: Newton backward interpolation formula

anchor.snippet: the Newton backward interpolation formula is expressed as

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Newton backward interpolation formula.

annotations.label: Lozenge diagram

anchor.snippet: A Lozenge diagram is a diagram that is used to describe different interpolation formulas

annotations.note: The combinatorial lozenge diagram for finite differences is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence of lozenge paths

anchor.snippet: Hence the equivalence of paths with same starting and ending points is shown

annotations.note: No formalization of lozenge-diagram path equivalence in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Newton formula from lozenge

anchor.snippet: gives the interpolation formula of all the

annotations.note: Mathlib does not derive Newton's formula via lozenge paths.

annotations.label: Gauss forward and backward formulas

anchor.snippet: we get Gauss forward formula

annotations.note: Gauss central-difference interpolation formulas are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stirling formula

anchor.snippet: we get Stirling formula

annotations.note: Stirling's interpolation formula is not in Mathlib (the asymptotic Stirling formula in Mathlib is a distinct concept).

annotations.label: Stirling as average of Gauss formulas

anchor.snippet: Stirling formula is the average of Gauss forward and Gauss backward formulas

annotations.note: No formalization of this relationship since the underlying formulas are not present.

annotations.label: Bessel formula

anchor.snippet: we get Bessel formula

annotations.note: Bessel's interpolation formula is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Runge function divergence

anchor.snippet: One classical example, due to Carl Runge

annotations.note: Runge's example/phenomenon does not appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Absolute value function example

anchor.snippet: Another example is the function

annotations.note: Bernstein's divergence example for |x| on equispaced nodes is not formalized.

annotations.label: Interpolation error formula

anchor.snippet: we get the error

annotations.note: No interpolation error formula `f(x) - p(x) = f^{(n)}(ξ)/n! · ∏(x - x_i)` is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lagrange remainder form of error

anchor.snippet: there is a Lagrange remainder form of the error

annotations.note: Mathlib has the Lagrange remainder for Taylor expansion (`taylor_mean_remainder_lagrange`) but not the analogous statement for Lagrange interpolation error.

annotations.label: Equally spaced error bound

anchor.snippet: the product term in the interpolation error formula can be bound

annotations.note: The bound on `∏(x - x_i)` for equispaced nodes is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lebesgue constant

anchor.snippet: The Lebesgue constant L is defined as the operator norm of X

annotations.note: The Lebesgue constant of an interpolation projector is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lebesgue's lemma bound

anchor.snippet: the interpolation polynomial is at most a factor

annotations.note: Lebesgue's lemma `‖f - p‖ ≤ (1 + Λ_n) E_n(f)` is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of converging nodes
annotations.note: Marcinkiewicz-type existence of a triangular node array giving uniform convergence is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Divergence for any fixed node table (Faber)

anchor.snippet: For any table of nodes there is a continuous function

annotations.note: Faber's theorem is not formalized (search for `Faber` returned no Mathlib decls).

annotations.label: Chebyshev nodes convergence for AC functions

anchor.snippet: For every absolutely continuous function

annotations.note: Convergence of Chebyshev-node interpolants for absolutely continuous functions is not present in Mathlib.
